page: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. This configuration provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations.

The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is delivered via a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps. The hardware profile represents a balanced resource density, offering qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring modern DDR5 memory speeds and dedicated compute resources without the overhead of local storage.

Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, where it achieves top-tier and strong ratings respectively. The presence of AMX instructions aids in LLM inference, with text generation for small models reaching top-tier speeds. Multi-threaded decompression and static web serving also register strong benchmark results. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, enterprise applications, web serving, text processing pipelines, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The processor features a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX). Benchmarks indicate strong performance in text processing, single-threaded compression, and small-model LLM inference. With a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps, this instance is well-suited for enterprise applications, web serving, data processing, and moderate inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose server with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, delivering strong performance in text processing and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong benchmark performance in text processing, compression, and LLM inference
Suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, and data pipelines
